Ten points in space, no three of which are collinear, are connected, each one
to all the others, by a total of $45$ line segments. The resulting framework
$F$ will be "disconnected" into two disjoint nonempty parts by the removal of
one point from the interior of each of the $9$ segments emanating from any one
vertex of $F$. Prove that $F$ cannot be similarly disconnected by the removal
of only $8$ points from the interiors of the $45$ segments.
